title: R66 full impl (sub-rules 4/5/8/9) — Owner & Phase decision

out_of_scope_deferred: R66.5 hijack detection (IP change / UA change / geo jump)
R66.8 login flood protection (per-IP / per-account rate limit)

pros: Clean room implementation — no carry-over from Tuần 2 prep
CMD AUTH spec can include full state machine for R66.4 multi-login + session.ts retirement
Foundation v2.8.0 implementation roadmap step 2 already lists 'CMD AUTH v1.0 (mới — chưa có trong 17 CMD list) — 45p'

B_cmd6.owner: CMD6 (currently cmd-place — TS Online analyst pivoted to world/place management)
cons: Scope mismatch — cmd-place owns map metadata + region binding, NOT auth/session
CMD6 has not implemented auth domain knowledge
B_cmd6.recommended_for: Only if Mr.Long wants to consolidate CMDs and pivot CMD6 again

pros: Continuity — em already built R66.1/2/3/6/7 in cmd-parse/auth/r66_session_token.ts
No CMD-onboarding overhead, can ship in ~Tuần 4 of Phase 14
Tests + tsc + audit infra already in place
cons: Stretches CMD4 charter ('parse + network + qa-core', NOT auth proper)
R66.5 hijack detection needs IP/UA/geo data pipeline — outside CMD4 scope
R66.8 login flood needs Redis-like rate-limiter — already in cmd-parse/output/anti_bot/redis_like.ts (PORTED but mock)
C_cmd4.recommended_for: If Mr.Long wants R66 finished THIS phase without CMD AUTH spinup

rationale: Foundation v2.8.0 explicitly lists CMD AUTH as separate new CMD (45p spec). Splitting R66 across 2 CMDs (CMD4 prep + CMD AUTH proper) is by-design grace period. CMD6 pivot is risky (already pivoted once: TS analyst → cmd-place). CMD4 extension is feasible but stretches charter.
decision_inputs_needed: Is Phase 15 close enough that A is acceptable, or is R66 a blocker for Phase 14 production?
If R66 must ship Phase 14: prefer C (CMD4 extension) over B (CMD6 third pivot).
Are R66.4-9 actually required for SVTK launch, or can MVP ship with R66.1/2/3/6 + legacy session.ts?
